danger_signals.visual_form: Music change + unique larger sprite with distinct appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Stage culmination threat; player must defeat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, holes, rivers, beds of spikes in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death zones; avoid or lose life

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite holding visible melee weapon (bat, whip, knife)
danger_signals.meaning: Increased threat level; disarm or take damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer display on HUD reaching zero
danger_signals.meaning: Stage failure imminent; forces progression pace

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar visual reduction on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability; death imminent if depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottom of screen boundary with bottomless pit visual
danger_signals.meaning: Knockback danger; fall causes instant death

safety_signals.visual_form: Automatic walking animation into next area after boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve; enemies retreat, level progressing safely
Buffer against death; temporary invulnerability purchased

safety_signals.visual_form: Rocks, oil drums, boxes visible in environment; melee weapons disarmed from enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Resource abundance; player can escalate offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Two-player co-op positioning on screen (Mode A); alternating turn gameplay
safety_signals.meaning: Shared threat; allies reduce individual vulnerability

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types with distinct sprites: Jack (stage 1 boss), Chin (stage 2 boss), Abobo (large muscular boss variant), Willy (final boss, gang leader), Linda (unique female fighter variant), generic underlings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gang hideout (stage 4) - interior fortress structure with traps; gang-controlled turf in city slum, factory, forest areas indicating territorial occupation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Twin martial artists (Billy and Jimmy Lee, also known as Hammer and Spike in supplementary materials)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Selectable character sprites in Mode B (Duel mode) with distinct appearance; Mode B features bigger sprites for selected character
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Progression through enemy territory indicates invasion/rescue mission narrative; starting from city slum moving inward toward hideout shows spatial hierarchy of enemy control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard enemy sprites positioned throughout scrolling level; multiple enemy types visible simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage player on contact; can grab player from behind, enabling coordinated attacks; can disarm and use weapons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Music changes; boss sprite appears (larger, distinct design); remaining underlings retreat from screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies except boss disengage; focus narrows to single threat; boss uses enhanced move set including potential throwing attacks (Abobo planned atomic throw)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Automatic transition cutscene; player character sprite walks forward into next stage; background shifts to new location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains passive momentum; no enemy interaction during transition; brief safety window before next stage threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior fortress setting (separate map from open-world stages); structural traps visible in geometry; final boss chamber with Willy waiting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Concentrated gauntlet of underlings; environmental hazards (dragon head statue with cracks, wall traps, falling stalactites implied); escalating danger progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger character sprites; selected character model appears for both P1 and P2 (except Abobo uses standard size); dedicated duel arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character stat differentiation: CPU opponents (except Linda) possess extra health; Abobo has substantially increased durability; player-vs-player symmetrical mechanics

environmental_storytelling.example: Level sequence (city slum → factory → forest → hideout) visually communicates enemy territory expansion; hideout as final interior space suggests gang command center; rescue mission through occupied territory

Jack, Chin, Abobo, Willy, Linda all have distinct sprite designs indicating rank/role within Black Warriors; larger/more ornate designs suggest leadership; Abobo's massive frame signals brute strength threat type

Stage 3 forest includes holes and rivers (natural obstacles); Stage 4 hideout includes constructed traps (spikes, falling hazards, dragon head statue) indicating deliberate fortress design and gang engineering capability
Baseball bats, whips, throwing knives, dynamite scattered throughout suggest gang armament; rocks, oil drums, boxes as makeshift weapons indicate improvised lower-tier threats vs equipped soldiers

Background music shift marks boss arrival without explicit narrative; intermission theme (cut off at 6 seconds) provides psychological tension before stage transitions; sonic pacing reinforces narrative momentum

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Unique boss sprite appears after music change; other enemies retreat; boss uses enhanced move set distinct from underlings
Has unused ladder-climbing animations; at stage end on construction site, if player climbs down, Chin remains still (behavioral artifact; likely incomplete AI for multi-level navigation)

Massive sprite size indicates power; planned genbaku nage (atomic throw) move in promotional materials but cut from final build; in Mode B duel, Abobo retains standard size but CPU variant has substantially increased health compared to other characters
Awaits player in main hall of hideout; unique narrative position as kidnapper/antagonist; visual centrality in fortress final chamber
Unique female sprite design; in CPU encounters, has standard health (not increased like other CPU opponents); visual differentiation suggests character role distinction within faction

Can grab player from behind in pairs, enabling coordinated multi-enemy attacks; can carry weapons (bats, whips, knives, dynamite); retreat when boss encounter initiates; reduced behavior complexity vs boss entities
Turn-based alternating control (not simultaneous co-op); when one player grabs enemy from behind, second player can attack unimpeded; represents mechanical cooperation without full screen presence

notes: Double Dragon NES deviates from arcade original with added EXP system (Mode A) and one-on-one duel mode (Mode B). Visual communication relies heavily on sprite differentiation, music state-changes, and environmental hazard design. Faction identity less about color/sigil and more about territorial spatial occupation and enemy type hierarchy. World-state shifts are communicated through automatic transitions and music cues rather than explicit narrative text. Unused graphics (staff roll, dragon head, Abobo throwing move) suggest richer environmental storytelling was planned but cut.
